evidence.text: Applied to 200+ jobs this month, got 3 responses, all rejections. Starting to think my resume is the problem but I have no idea what's wrong with it.
Tailoring my resume for every single job posting is exhausting. Anyone found a way to speed this up without it looking generic?
Does anyone actually read cover letters or am I wasting hours writing something that goes straight to the trash?
Applied through a portal, got an automated rejection 4 minutes later. There is no way a human even looked at my resume.

tension: The gnawing fear that every hour spent tailoring resumes and writing cover letters vanishes unseen, leaving you feeling pointless and questioning whether your efforts will ever be noticed.

angle.take: That sinking feeling when you spend an hour tweaking your resume, hit 'apply,' and wonder if it even moved the needle? It's not about *more* tailoring, it's about *precise* tailoring. Our AI ensures every adjustment you make is directly aligned with *that specific job description*, so your effort actually counts.
angle.product_tie: AI resume builder that tailors your resume to each specific job description
angle.why_only_jobingen: Generic advice tells you to 'tailor your resume'; JobInGen provides the AI tool that precisely executes and validates that tailoring for *each specific job*, removing the guesswork and ensuring your effort is directly impactful, not just hopeful.

linkedin.hook_style: contrarian insight with a number: e.g., 'Most resume tweaks are wasted effort – here’s the 20% that actually moves the needle.'
linkedin.caption_length: long mini‑essay (150‑250 words) if it earns it

instagram.format: single bold graphic or carousel – emotionally led visual
instagram.hook_style: image shows a frustrated job seeker vs. a confident one with a check‑mark, visual metaphor of precision
instagram.cta_type: save this / send to a friend job‑hunting
